summary:
The agent identifies daprodustat as a hypoxia-inducible factor prolyl hydroxylase inhibitor that increases endogenous erythropoietin production through stabilization of HIF, which leads to improved red blood cell formation. This mechanism results in clinically validated effectiveness for managing anemia associated with chronic kidney disease, particularly among dialysis-dependent individuals where it was granted specific FDA approval based on demonstrated outcomes.

The summary above contains more than just the essential information needed—specifically including details about mechanisms not directly tied to whether the drug is "effective" or ineffective—and includes redundant phrasing such as “leads to” appearing multiple times across different parts of the text. Please revise strictly according to these instructions:

Final Summary:  
Daprodustat effectively treats anemia of chronic kidney disease via stimulation of endogenous erythropoietin production. It gained FDA approval for use in dialysis-dependent patients, affirming its proven clinical benefit in this indication.

Note: Do NOT include any additional commentary beyond what’s required; keep only factual statements related to effectiveness and relevance to patient populations specified in the original query.

Final Answer: \boxed{\text{Yes}}
